definition: The degree to which a product satisfies a strong market demand.
simple_explanation: When people are buying your product as fast as you can make it, or using it as fast as you can add servers.
investor_explanation: We believe we have achieved early PMF because our organic growth rate is 15% month-over-month and our user retention curve flattens out at 40%.
examples: Before scaling the sales team, we need to ensure we have true product-market fit.
The survey showed that 50% of users would be 'very disappointed' if they could no longer use our app, indicating strong PMF.
questions: How are you measuring product-market fit?
Who is your ideal customer profile?
What evidence do you have that people actually want this?

definition: The amount of time a company has before it runs out of cash, assuming current income and expenses remain constant.
simple_explanation: How many months your startup can survive on the money it currently has in the bank.
investor_explanation: With our current burn rate of $50k per month and $1M in the bank, we have 20 months of runway, which gives us plenty of time to reach our Series A milestones.
examples: We need to raise a bridge round to extend our runway by 6 months.
Cutting our marketing spend increased our runway significantly.
questions: How will this funding round impact your runway?
What happens if revenue drops by 50%?

definition: An attribute that enables a company to outperform its competitors.
simple_explanation: The special thing you do better than anyone else that makes it hard for rivals to copy you.
investor_explanation: Our durable competitive advantage is our proprietary dataset; while competitors rely on open-source data, our models are trained on exclusive, industry-specific records.
examples: Their deep integration with legacy banking systems serves as a strong competitive advantage.
We need to clearly communicate our competitive advantage in the pitch deck.
questions: Why can't Google or Microsoft just copy this?
How will your competitive advantage scale as you grow?
